sections.content:
Clicking on the green square did nothing (board changed: False).
Clicking on the yellow square changed the board (board changed: True). Let me check what changed.
The yellow square is still at rows 20-21, cols 24-25. But the board changed. Let me check what other objects changed.
Looking at the image, I see the left panel has changed. Let me check the ASCII to see what changed.
Actually, I realize I need to check the last transition to see what changed when I clicked on the yellow square.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Clicking yellow square changed the board. Let me see what changed.
# Check last transition
if last_transition:
    before_seg = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
    after_seg = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation
    
    # Compare node positions
    before_nodes = {n['color']: n for n in before_seg['nodes'] if n['pixels'] < 100}
    after_nodes = {n['color']: n for n in after_seg['nodes'] if n['pixels'] < 100}
    
    print("Objects that changed:")
    for color in set(list(before_nodes.keys()) + list(after_nodes.keys())):
        if color in before_nodes and color in after_nodes:
            b = before_nodes[color]
            a = after_nodes[color]
            if b['boundary'] != a['boundary']:
                print(f"  {color}: {b['boundary']} -> {a['boundary']}")
        elif color in after_nodes:
            print(f"  {color}: appeared at {after_nodes[color]['boundary']}")
        else:
            print(f"  {color}: disappeared from {before_nodes[color]['boundary']}")

# Check current ASCII
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nLeft panel (rows 18-45, cols 6-26):")
for i in range(18, 45):
    print(f"  {i}: {ascii_lines[i][6:26]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

- Blue block (bottom)

- Blue block at rows 34-37 (bottom)

Actually, let me reconsider. The initial state had:

- Blue/charcoal pattern at rows 34-37, cols 8-11

2. Moved the red block from rows 30-33 to rows 24-29
3. Swapped the blue block and blue/charcoal pattern
And clicking on the right panel's blue block:
1. Toggled the blue block and blue/charcoal pattern back and forth
Wait, I need to re-check. Let me look at the history to see what happened after each click.
